title: Governed Transition and Conflict Engine
description: Govern correction, refinement, expansion, restriction, supersession, reversal, retraction, context shift, confidence change, merge, split, and deprecation as journaled transitions between exact knowledge-state versions, including conflict, branching, and review semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Invalid, cyclic, contradictory, or unauthorized transitions fail closed
Explicit subject declarations, human-reviewed conclusions, and governed inference remain distinguishable
Every transition binds exact before-and-after hashes and produces an immutable receipt
Branch, merge, split, overlap, and conflicting-authority conditions are explicit and reconstructable
Human-state transitions run through the generic engine while retaining stricter human policy controls
